content:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding custom filters support to prompt decorator (filters param, create_jinja_env helper)

fields.summary: Adding custom filters support to prompt decorator (filters param, create_jinja_env helper)

content:
type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader]=None, filters: Optional[Dict[str,Callable]]=None) -> jinja2.Environment; builds env, sets builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None at the end so local filters win. Hook your global registry merge in between builtins and the local  update (so precedence is: builtins < global registry < per-call filters). Function lives right after imports in outlines/prompts.py, called from Prompt._template_from_str and _template_from_file.

content:
type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment. It builds the env, sets builtin filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args), then does env.filters.update(filters) at the end if filters is not None, so local (per-call) filters win. Please hook your global registry merge in between the builtins and that final update, so precedence is builtins < global registry < per-call filters. It lives right after the imports in outlines/prompts.py and is called from Prompt._template_from_str and _template_from_file.

fields.summary: create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment. It builds the env, sets builtin filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args), then does env.filters.update(filters) at the end if filters is not None, so local (per-call) filters win. Please hook your global registry merge in between the builtins and that final update, so precedence is builtins < global registry < per-call filters. It lives right after the imports in outlines/prompts.py and is called from Prompt._template_from_str and _template_from_file.
